2026-04-23T06:57:59.4548879Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T06:57:59.4549194Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T06:57:59.4549482Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T06:57:59.4549727Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T06:57:59.4549908Z [36;1m[0m

The `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` package just hit its first stable release `1.0.0` in PR #58546, but its README lacks service-specific key concepts and code examples. 2026-04-23T07:03:49.5870879Z - The README is still a boilerplate template with no service-specific Key Concepts and an Examples section that only links to a generic page.
2026-04-23T07:03:51.6563768Z - Four test sample files exist in `tests/Samples/` but lack `#region Snippet:` markers and are not wired to the README.
2026-04-23T07:03:52.8563859Z - Issue filed with a full implementation guide: new `Sample_HelloWorld.cs` with snippet regions for create/list/get/delete, updated README Key Concepts and Examples sections, and validation steps.
2026-04-23T07:03:52.8584024Z - Triage dispatched for labeling and routing.

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match","enum":["stars","forks","help-wanted-issues","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_repositories"}]}}}
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0218477Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T07:03:29.729993655Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #58546 (commit 6bc663352) by @ArthurMa1978\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58546 promoted `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` from `1.0.0-beta.1` to its first stable GA release `1.0.0`. The CHANGELOG entry for `1.0.0` only notes dependency upgrades — it does not describe the service's resources or capabilities. The README is still a boilerplate copy of the ARM template with no service-specific content or code samples.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The `Key concepts` section contains only a generic link to Azure SDK docs; it does not explain the core resources (`DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsPoolCollection`) or the key abstractions (organization profiles for Azure DevOps vs GitHub, agent profiles for stateless/stateful agents, VM fabric profiles)\n- The `Examples` section contains only a link to a generic samples page — no inline code demonstrating how to create, list, get, or delete a `DevOpsPool`\n- Four test sample files exist in `tests/Samples/` (`Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, `Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs`) but none contain `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README\n- The CHANGELOG for `1.0.0` describes no features for end users of the stable release (it only mentions dependency bumps from the beta)\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — **edit**\n\nReplace the boilerplate `Key concepts` section (currently just a link) with service-specific content:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` library lets you manage **Managed DevOps Pools** — fully managed Azure DevOps agent pools backed by Azure virtual machines.\n\n### Core resources\n\n| Resource | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `DevOpsPoolResource` | Represents a single Managed DevOps Pool. |\n| `DevOpsPoolCollection` | Collection of pools scoped to a resource group. |\n\n### Organization profiles\n\nPools can serve either Azure DevOps organizations (`DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`) or GitHub organizations (`DevOpsGitHubOrganizationProfile`).\n\n### Agent profiles\n\n- `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile` — agents are fresh for each job (recommended for most workloads).\n- `DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile` — agents retain state across jobs for faster warm-up.\n- `AutomaticResourcePredictionsProfile` — the service automatically predicts and pre-provisions agents.\n\n### Fabric profiles\n\n`DevOpsVmssFabricProfile` specifies the VM SKU, OS images, network configuration, and OS settings used for the pool's virtual machines.\n\nFor general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts, see the [Azure SDK for .NET documentation](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html).\n```\n\nReplace the boilerplate `Examples` section with snippet placeholders:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\n### Create a Managed DevOps Pool\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\n```\n```\n\n### List pools in a resource group\n\n```C# Snippet:ListDevOpsPools\n```\n```\n\n### Get a pool by name\n\n```C# Snippet:GetDevOpsPool\n```\n```\n\n### Delete a pool\n\n```C# Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool\n```\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nWrite a new test sample file at:\n`sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_HelloWorld.cs`\n\nUse the operations already demonstrated in `Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs` and `Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs` as the basis. The snippet names must match the README placeholders above.\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Core;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure.Models;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure.Samples\n{\n    public partial class Sample_HelloWorld\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task CreateDevOpsPool()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\n\n            DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\n\n            string poolName = \"myPool\";\n            DevOpsPoolData data = new DevOpsPoolData(new AzureLocation(\"eastus\"))\n            {\n                Properties = new DevOpsPoolProperties(\n                    maximumConcurrency: 4,\n                    organizationProfile: new DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile(new[]\n                    {\n                        new DevOpsOrganization(new Uri(\"https://dev.azure.com/myOrg\")) { OpenAccess = true }\n                    }),\n                    agentProfile: new DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile(),\n                    fabricProfile: new DevOpsVmssFabricProfile(\"Standard_D2ads_v5\", new[]\n                    {\n                        new DevOpsPoolVmImage { ResourceId = \"/MicrosoftWindowsServer/WindowsServer/2022-datacenter/latest\" }\n                    }),\n                    devCenterProjectResourceId: \"/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.DevCenter/projects/myProject\")\n            };\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cDevOpsPoolResource\u003e lro = await collection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, poolName, data);\n            DevOpsPoolResource pool = lro.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Pool created: {pool.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task ListDevOpsPools()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:ListDevOpsPools\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\n\n            DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools();\n\n            await foreach (DevOpsPoolResource pool in collection.GetAllAsync())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine($\"Pool: {pool.Data.Name}, Location: {pool.Data.Location}\");\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task GetDevOpsPool()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:GetDevOpsPool\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\n\n            DevOpsPoolResource pool = await resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPoolAsync(\"myPool\");\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Pool name: {pool.Data.Name}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task DeleteDevOpsPool()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupId);\n\n            DevOpsPoolResource pool = await resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPoolAsync(\"myPool\");\n            await pool.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\n            Console.WriteLine(\"Pool deleted.\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) are present and adequate.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order. 2026-04-23T07:03:57.0527068Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0527199Z **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure`
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0527413Z **Service directory:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/`
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0527529Z **Triggered by:** PR #58546 (commit 6bc663352) by @ArthurMa1978
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0527532Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0527593Z ## What Changed
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0527602Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0528611Z PR #58546 promoted `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` from `1.0.0-beta.1` to its first stable GA release `1.0.0`. The CHANGELOG entry for `1.0.0` only notes dependency upgrades — it does not describe the service's resources or capabilities. The README is still a boilerplate copy of the ARM template with no service-specific content or code samples.
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0528620Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0528684Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0528794Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0529589Z - The `Key concepts` section contains only a generic link to Azure SDK docs; it does not explain the core resources (`DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsPoolCollection`) or the key abstractions (organization profiles for Azure DevOps vs GitHub, agent profiles for stateless/stateful agents, VM fabric profiles)
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0530000Z - The `Examples` section contains only a link to a generic samples page — no inline code demonstrating how to create, list, get, or delete a `DevOpsPool`
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0530769Z - Four test sample files exist in `tests/Samples/` (`Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, `Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs`) but none contain `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531052Z - The CHANGELOG for `1.0.0` describes no features for end users of the stable release (it only mentions dependency bumps from the beta)
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531062Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531120Z <details>
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531303Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531306Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531551Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531555Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531626Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531629Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531911Z **File:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — **edit**
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0531914Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532125Z Replace the boilerplate `Key concepts` section (currently just a link) with service-specific content:
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532128Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532198Z ```markdown
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532282Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532285Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532770Z The `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` library lets you manage **Managed DevOps Pools** — fully managed Azure DevOps agent pools backed by Azure virtual machines.
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532782Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532849Z ### Core resources
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532852Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532927Z | Resource | Description |
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0532989Z |---|---|
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533130Z | `DevOpsPoolResource` | Represents a single Managed DevOps Pool. |
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533292Z | `DevOpsPoolCollection` | Collection of pools scoped to a resource group. |
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533296Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533365Z ### Organization profiles
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533368Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533711Z Pools can serve either Azure DevOps organizations (`DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`) or GitHub organizations (`DevOpsGitHubOrganizationProfile`).
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533715Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533776Z ### Agent profiles
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0533779Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0534050Z - `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile` — agents are fresh for each job (recommended for most workloads).
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0534277Z - `DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile` — agents retain state across jobs for faster warm-up.
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0534579Z - `AutomaticResourcePredictionsProfile` — the service automatically predicts and pre-provisions agents.
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0534583Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0534645Z ### Fabric profiles
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0534648Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0534951Z `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile` specifies the VM SKU, OS images, network configuration, and OS settings used for the pool's virtual machines.
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0534954Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0535316Z For general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts, see the [Azure SDK for .NET documentation](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html).
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0535376Z ```
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0535379Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0535528Z Replace the boilerplate `Examples` section with snippet placeholders:
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0535533Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0535593Z ```markdown
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0535649Z ## Examples
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0535733Z

2026-04-23T07:03:57.0537113Z 
2026-04-23T07:03:57.0537224Z ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets
